methodsAttempted.query: site:louisville.edu University of Louisville generative AI policy ChatGPT academic integrity teaching guidance
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the Delphi Center generative AI teaching page, library AI guides, a School of Medicine policy PDF, and course/research-only results.

methodsAttempted.query: site:louisville.edu UofL GenAI Guidelines AI accounts sharing information university policy data privacy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found ITS AI Culture, Delphi teaching guidance, a Learn More About AI page, and the School of Medicine policy PDF.

methodsAttempted.query: site:louisville.edu University of Louisville AI citation ChatGPT library citation generative AI academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found Delphi sample syllabus statements, the Brand AI Content Policy, library/tool pages, and writing-center plagiarism-detection policy.

methodsAttempted.query: site:louisville.edu University of Louisville generative AI approved tools Copilot ChatGPT data classification policy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the TeamDynamix AI / GenAI Tools service entry, Brand AI Content Policy, Delphi guidance, library guides, and ITS AI Culture.

Verified UofL-controlled hosts used in this bundle: delphi.louisville.edu, louisville.edu/its, brand.louisville.edu, and service.louisville.edu. robots.txt allowed the fetched public evidence paths; louisville.edu/its/services/ai returned 403 and is recorded as inaccessible.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery located central UofL AI teaching guidance from the Delphi Center, sample syllabus statements, ITS AI culture and data-handling guidance, a TeamDynamix AI / GenAI Tools service entry, and a Brand AI Content Policy page that is explicitly marked as a draft awaiting approval. Claims are scoped to these source limits. Library resource/training pages, a School of Medicine policy PDF, an ITS approved-tools page that returned 403, and a course syllabus were rejected or caveated because they are inaccessible, low-specificity, training-only, school-specific, or course-specific.

stopConditions: Stop after verified official UofL sources support narrow claims for teaching, academic integrity, privacy/security, access process, and caveated draft brand guidance.
Do not crawl login-only SharePoint, Blackboard, or other credentialed resources.
Do not use course-specific or school-specific sources for university-wide claims.

artifacts.errorReason: 403 Forbidden; not authorized to access this resource.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Louisville Delphi Center frames generative and agentic AI as a teaching and assessment-design issue, advising instructors to design assessments so authentic engagement is more valuable than AI substitution rather than focusing on policing student behavior.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Louisville Delphi Center says current AI detection tools are unreliable, raise data privacy, FERPA, and student-consent risks, and should never be the sole basis for an academic integrity decision.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Louisville Delphi Center provides sample syllabus statements for three generative AI approaches: not permitted, permitted with prior instructor permission and appropriate attribution and citation, or allowed without restrictions.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UofL ITS AI guidance states that federal privacy regulations and the NIST AI Risk Management Framework should be followed, and that users are individually responsible for misuse of systems and data mishandling involving student data, intellectual property, and protected personal information.

citation.publisher: University of Louisville Information Technology Services
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The UofL service catalog describes an AI / GenAI Tools access request service, but marks it as coming soon and not currently available; the described process would limit access to approved university platforms and use cases and require intended use, data sensitivity, and compliance considerations.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UofL's Brand Identity site publishes an Artificial Intelligence Content Policy page for university brand communications, but the page explicitly states that it is a draft policy awaiting approval by the University of Louisville administration.

citation.publisher: University of Louisville Brand Identity & Visual Standards
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Rather than asking whether a student used AI, it asks whether the assessment is designed in a way that makes authentic engagement more valuable than AI substitution(Su et al., 2024).
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The guidance frames assessment design around making authentic engagement more valuable than AI substitution.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Current AI detection tools are unreliable and frequently produce false positives that disproportionately affect multilingual writers and students who use writing tools such as grammar checkers or the word prediction feature in Microsoft Word.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The page says AI detection tools are unreliable and can produce false positives, especially affecting multilingual writers and users of writing tools.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: On the Use of AI Detection Tools

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow teaching-guidance claim. It does not assert a binding student policy.
Approved because the source directly says AI detection outputs should never serve as the sole basis for an academic integrity decision.
Approved as sample syllabus language only, not as a single university-wide AI permission rule.
Approved as ITS privacy/security guidance. It does not list a specific approved tool.
Approved as source-status because the service page is marked coming soon and not currently available.
Approved only as a draft-policy source-status claim. The bundle does not present the draft brand page as binding approved policy.

artifacts.title: University of Louisville AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: The crawl found official University of Louisville sources covering generative AI teaching and assessment design, AI detection caveats for academic integrity, sample syllabus statements, ITS privacy/security principles for AI, a coming-soon AI / GenAI tools access service, and a Brand AI Content Policy page explicitly marked as draft awaiting approval. Claims are agent-reviewed candidates only and are scoped to each source's wording.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The Brand AI Content Policy source is explicitly marked as a draft awaiting approval and should not be treated as an approved binding policy without later review.
The official ITS services AI page returned 403 Forbidden and was not used as claim evidence.
Library resource/training pages, a School of Medicine policy PDF, and course-specific syllabus material were not used to infer university-wide policy.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
